fitness_rules: If BMI is over 25, prioritize calorie deficit and consistency in walking.
For 'Poor' fitness level, start with 15 minutes of joint-friendly yoga like Tadasana.
For 'Fit' fitness level, incorporate high-intensity interval training (HIIT) to maintain metabolic health.
Cardiovascular health is improved by maintaining a steady heart rate during brisk walking or cycling.
diet_knowledge: A vegetarian diet for fitness should include high-protein sources like Paneer, Dal, Soya Chunks, and Greek Yogurt.
Non-Vegetarian protein sources like Chicken Breast, Fish, and Egg Whites are excellent for muscle recovery.
Managing Cholesterol involves avoiding trans fats and incorporating high-fiber foods like Oats and Flax seeds.
Lowering Blood Pressure can be aided by a low-sodium diet and potassium-rich foods like Bananas and Spinach.
exercise_science: Strength training twice a week helps in maintaining bone density and increasing resting metabolism.
Yoga poses like Bhujangasana (Cobra Pose) can improve spinal flexibility and respiratory capacity.
Consistency is more important than intensity when starting a new fitness journey for beginners.
